title: Spot open redirect via URI.parse allowlist with scheme gap
prompt:
This function tries to validate a redirect URL via `URI.parse/1` and a host allowlist, but forgets to reject `javascript:` and `data:` schemes:

```elixir
@hosts ["example.com", "dashboard.example.com"]

def safe_redirect(conn, url) do
  case URI.parse(url) do
    %URI{host: host} when host in @hosts ->
      redirect(conn, external: url)
    _ ->
      redirect(conn, to: "/")
  end
end
```

Rewrite this so the scheme is checked against `["https", "http"]` (rejecting `javascript:`, `data:`, `file:`, etc.), the host is still checked against the allowlist, and if ANY check fails the redirect goes to `/`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/safe_redirect.ex`. The fix must include a scheme check that explicitly allows only `"https"` or `"http"`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires catching that host-allowlist alone isn't enough; scheme must also be checked.
